# Step 11 â€” Effector Activation by FcÎ³R {#step11}

The chain analysis treated ADCD as the effector node. Here we ask the same activation question of all
three antibody-dependent effector functions â€” complement deposition (ADCD), cellular phagocytosis
(ADCP), and neutrophil phagocytosis (ADNP): **is each engaged by FcÎ³R2a/FcÎ³R3b?** For every effector
present for a given antigen we regress the effector on the two receptors, isolate the FcÎ³R3b-specific
contribution (residual on FcÎ³R2a), and test whether the FcÎ³Râ†’effector mapping differs by maternal arm.
ADCP and ADNP are available for only a subset of antigens; absent effectorÃ—antigen cells are skipped.

```{r step11-run, results='asis'}
.stage(11, "effector activation by FcÎ³R (ADCD / ADCP / ADNP)")
if (!RUN_EFF_ACT) {
  cat("*Step 11 skipped (run_effector_activation = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    rows <- list(); irows <- list()
    for (fn in EFFECTORS) {
      for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
        eff <- paste0(A, "_", fn); r2a <- paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"); r3b <- paste0(A, "_FcgR3b")
        if (!all(c(eff, r2a, r3b) %in% names(wd))) next
        for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
          d <- arm_data_m(mode, m) %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(eff, r2a, r3b))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
          if (nrow(d) < NETWORK_MIN) next
          f <- lm(reformulate(c(r2a, r3b), response = eff), data = d)
          co <- summary(f)$coefficients
          d$.fres <- resid(lm(reformulate(r2a, response = r3b), data = d))
          base <- lm(reformulate(r2a, response = eff), data = d)
          full <- lm(reformulate(c(r2a, ".fres"), response = eff), data = d)
          uc <- summary(full)$coefficients[".fres", ]
          rows[[length(rows)+1]] <- data.frame(
            effector = fn, antigen = A, arm = m, n = nrow(d),
            R2 = round(summary(f)$r.squared, 3),
            "Î² FcÎ³R2a" = round(co[r2a,1],3), "p FcÎ³R2a" = signif(co[r2a,4],3),
            "Î² FcÎ³R3b" = round(co[r3b,1],3), "p FcÎ³R3b" = signif(co[r3b,4],3),
            "3b|2a Î”RÂ²" = round(summary(full)$r.squared - summary(base)$r.squared, 3),
            "3b|2a p" = signif(uc[4],3), check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
        }
        # arm interaction on the FcÎ³R->effector mapping (pooled, standardized predictors)
        di <- wd %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(eff, r2a, r3b, "arm_name"))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
        if (nrow(di) >= 40) {
          di[[r2a]] <- as.numeric(scale(di[[r2a]])); di[[r3b]] <- as.numeric(scale(di[[r3b]]))
          di$arm <- factor(di$arm_name, levels = cfg$maternal_arms)
          f_add <- lm(reformulate(c(r2a, r3b, "arm"), response = eff), data = di)
          f_int <- lm(stats::as.formula(paste0(eff, " ~ (", r2a, " + ", r3b, ") * arm")), data = di)
          av <- anova(f_add, f_int)
          irows[[length(irows)+1]] <- data.frame(
            effector = fn, antigen = A, n = nrow(di),
            "blockÃ—arm F" = round(av$F[2],2), "blockÃ—arm p" = signif(av$`Pr(>F)`[2],3),
            check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
        }
        .info("step11 [%s] %s %s activation done", mode, fn, A)
      }
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (is.null(tb)) { cat("*No effectorÃ—antigen present in this mode.*\n\n"); next }
    print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Effector activation by FcÎ³R [", mode,
      "]: effector regressed on FcÎ³R2a + FcÎ³R3b, with the FcÎ³R3b-specific (residual-on-2a) contribution."))); cat("\n")
    ib <- do.call(rbind, irows)
    if (!is.null(ib)) print(knitr::kable(ib, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Arm-modification of the FcÎ³Râ†’effector mapping [", mode,
      "]: F-test of the FcÎ³R-block Ã— arm interaction (reference ", cfg$maternal_arms[1], ")."))); cat("\n")
  }
}
```

**How to read this.** A significant positive FcÎ³R coefficient means that effector is antibody-Fc-driven
in that arm/antigen; comparing the three functions shows whether FcÎ³R engagement feeds complement
(ADCD), monocyte phagocytosis (ADCP) and neutrophil phagocytosis (ADNP) equally or selectively. The
`3b|2a` columns isolate the FcÎ³R3b-specific drive (the receptor implicated for SBA in the ADCD
chain). A significant blockÃ—arm interaction indicates the FcÎ³Râ†’effector coupling itself differs
between TdaP and TT infants, not just its inputs.
